channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Companies Fit Build Investor Confidence Through Media and Leadership Visibility
item.description: For any company wey dey try attract investors, na long before the quarterly meetings or investor calls that investor dey make decision. Investor confidence dey start from the kind story wey dey form a...

item.title: Prince Edward Island RCMP identify human remains from three decades ago using familial DNA
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) in Prince Edward Island have identified human remains that were discovered almost 30 years ago through the use of familial DNA technology. The remains, initial...

item.title: How Mobile Gamers Now Prefer Guides Before Playing
item.description: Mobile entertainment don change, no be like before wey you fit just open phone, tap app, and kill ten minutes. Nowadays, players dey waka between short videos, sports updates, casual games, betting sl...

item.title: Rising Iodine Deficiency Linked to Changing Salt Choices and Diets
item.description: Iodine deficiency, once considered a solved public health issue, is now slowly resurfacing in several countries due to shifts in dietary habits and salt consumption patterns. The iodisation of salt du...

item.title: Trump Delays Military Strike on Iran as Peace Talks Continue
item.description: President Trump announced on Monday that he was calling off a planned U.S. military attack on Iran, originally scheduled for Tuesday, citing ongoing 'serious negotiations' toward a peace deal. The ann...

item.title: Senior Bureau of Meteorology executive steps down following troubled website rollout
item.description: Peter Stone, a senior executive at the Bureau of Meteorology (BoM), is stepping down after nearly a decade with the organisation. His tenure has been marked by controversy, including a close associati...

item.title: Key Developments in Philippine Politics and Global Affairs on May 19, 2026
item.description: On May 19, 2026, the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Philippine National Police (PNP) clarified that the incident on May 13 did not constitute an attack on the Senate. I...

item.title: San Diego mosque shooting victims include heroic husband and devoted caretaker
item.description: A tragic shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday has claimed the lives of several individuals, including Nader Awad, 57, a husband who attempted to protect his wife and students, and Man...

item.title: Conservative Leader Poilievre's Chief of Staff Ian Todd to Retire, Steve Outhouse Named Successor

item.title: Mobile Evening Clinics Bring Essential Healthcare to Seasonal Farm Workers in Nkqubela
item.description: In Nkqubela township near Robertson in the Western Cape, community health workers have introduced mobile evening clinics every Thursday to help seasonal farm workers who cannot access daytime healthca...

item.title: Oregon Senate Primary 2026: Jeff Merkley Seeks Democratic Nomination
item.description: Senator Jeff Merkley, a Democrat who has represented Oregon in the U.S. Senate since 2009, is seeking reelection in the 2026 Senate race. The state, traditionally leaning Democratic, will see Merkley ...

item.title: Surrey Police investigate historical child sexual abuse allegations following Epstein files release
item.description: Surrey Police has confirmed it is investigating two separate allegations of non-recent child sexual abuse following the recent release of documents related to the convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein ...

item.title: Man killed and four injured in Sydney south-west shooting incident
item.description: A tragic shooting in Sydney's south-west has left one man dead and four others injured. NSW Police were first called to Liverpool Hospital at approximately 8.30pm on Tuesday after two men, believed to...

item.title: Moving from Exhaustion to Flow: Embracing Life with Honesty and Rhythm
item.description: Allan Weppenaar Pedersen’s book, GAIA: Let The River Flow, explores the shift from constant stress and exhaustion towards a state of flow. The narrative focuses on Jonas, a relatable character who per...

item.title: Two American tourists arrested for trespassing into Punch the monkey's enclosure at Ichikawa City Zoo in Japan
item.description: Two American tourists were arrested in Japan after one of them climbed into the enclosure of Punch, a popular baby macaque at the Ichikawa City Zoo. Reid Jahnai Daysun, 24, entered the Saruyama monkey...

item.title: Radio host questions Prime Minister Anthony Albanese about domestic violence policies
item.description: The news article reports on a radio interview in which Prime Minister Anthony Albanese was questioned about domestic violence issues. The host pressed the Prime Minister on the government's policies a...

item.title: Mbalula stresses independence ahead of 2027 ANC conference, calls for party renewal
item.description: ANC secretary-general Fikile Mbalula has addressed growing tensions within the party, asserting that he will not be swayed by internal criticism ahead of the 2027 ANC elective conference. Speaking at ...

item.title: Pep Guardiola addresses speculation over Manchester City future ahead of Bournemouth clash
item.description: Pep Guardiola has dismissed rumours regarding his potential departure from Manchester City, stating that such speculation had no impact on the team's preparations for their Premier League fixture agai...

item.title: Independent Review Finds Issues with Churchill Falls MOU, Questions Public Interest
item.description: An independent review committee has released a report on the Churchill Falls memorandum of understanding (MOU) between Newfoundland and Labrador and Quebec, concluding that the agreement has fundament...
